name: Canned Foods for Special Diets
recipeIngredient: Commercially canned special diet food cost
Fully ripe but firm, best quality fruits for canning without sugar
Water or regular unsweetened fruit juices (e.g., apple, pineapple, white grape) for canning without sugar
Tomatoes, vegetables, meats, poultry, and seafood for canning without salt
Salt substitutes for seasoning when serving
recipeInstructions:
1. For canning regular fruits without sugar: Select fully ripe but firm, best quality fruits. Prepare as hot-packs using water or unsweetened fruit juices instead of sugar syrup. Use juice made from the fruit being canned; blends of unsweetened apple, pineapple, and white grape juice work well for filling over solid fruit pieces. Adjust headspaces and lids according to regular fruit processing recommendations. Add sugar substitutes when serving.
2. For canning tomatoes, vegetables, meats, poultry, and seafood without salt: Follow normal canning procedures but omit salt. Salt is only used to season the food; it's not necessary for safety. Add salt substitutes when serving.
